perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall not enter into any agreement referred to in this Article 2 with any Redistributor or Customer until each such agreement has been submitted to and approved by Licensor. Within five (5) business days after its receipt of any such agreement, Licensor shall notify Licensee whether it approves or disapproves of the agreement and, if it disapproves of the agreement, Licensor shall provide written notice of the reasons therefor, including any changes that would require to approve of the agreement. If Licensor fails to notify Licensee of its approval or disapproval of any such agreement within such period of time, the agreement shall be deemed to be approved by Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall not enter into any agreement referred to in this Article 2 with any Redistributor or Customer until each such agreement has been submitted to and approved by Licensor. The Licensor's approval is not required for agreements with Customers only Redistributors. Within five (5) business days after its receipt of any such agreement, Licensor shall notify Licensee whether it approves or disapproves of the agreement and, if it disapproves of the agreement, Licensor shall provide written notice of the reasons therefor, including any changes that would require to approve of the agreement. If Licensor fails to notify Licensee of its approval or disapproval of any such agreement within such period of time, the agreement shall be deemed to be approved by Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence contradicts the original requirement for Licensor approval of ALL agreements (Redistributor and Customer). Now, customer agreements can be made without Licensor approval. This creates a contradiction that impacts how Section 2.6 is enforced because it is unclear if customer agreements must still adhere to restrictions of Section 2.6 (a) Diminish or limit any of the rights of Licensor in the Licensed Products or Documentation;(b) Diminish or limit the enforceability of the proprietary rights of Licensor in and to the Licensed Products or Documentation; (c) Convey any rights of ownership in the Licensed Products or Documentation to any individual or entity other than Licensor, except for the license rights granted in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (d) Permit the use or duplication of the Licensed Products or Documentation, except as specifically provided in this Agreement or in the Sublicense; or (e) Permit disclosure of proprietary information regarding the Licensed Products or Documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee shall ensure that the terms of any Redistributor Agreement and, to the extent a Sublicense must be modified to comply with applicable law, any Sublicense executed in connection with the Licensed Products do not: (a) Diminish or limit any of the rights of Licensor in the Licensed Products or Documentation; (b) Diminish or limit the enforceability of the proprietary rights of Licensor in and to the Licensed Products or Documentation; (c) Convey any rights of ownership in the Licensed Products or Documentation to any individual or entity other than Licensor, except for the license rights granted in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (d) Permit the use or duplication of the Licensed Products or Documentation, except as specifically provided in this Agreement or in the Sublicense; or (e) Permit disclosure of proprietary information regarding the Licensed Products or Documentation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that Licensor approval is not required for agreements with Customers, only Redistributors. The contradicted text requires that the Licensee ensures terms of any Redistributor Agreement do not diminish the rights of the Licensor in the Licensed Products. This contradiction arises because the original text in the first section required Licensor approval for ALL agreements (Redistributor and Customer) and the contradicted text enforces conditions on 'any Redistributor Agreement' which could now be seen to exclude customer agreements due to the inserted sentence. Therefore, customer agreements are no longer required to adhere to these restrictions, leading to a direct contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall pay to Licensor for each Licensed Product licensed to a Redistributor or a Customer a licensee fee equal to 50% of all revenues received (without deduction for value added tax, if any, but excluding any revenues for maintenance and support or upgrade services, which revenues are covered in paragraph (b) below) by Licensee under the Redistributor Agreement or Sublicense applicable to such Licensed Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall pay to Licensor for each Licensed Product licensed to a Redistributor a licensee fee equal to 50% of all revenues received (without deduction for value added tax, if any, but excluding any revenues for maintenance and support or upgrade services, which revenues are covered in paragraph (b) below) by Licensee under the Redistributor Agreement or Sublicense applicable to such Licensed Product. Licensee shall retain 100% of the revenue from customer.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation removes the licensee fee obligation for direct customers, thus potentially contradicting the following section that still accounts for Licensee paying Licensor a percentage for maintenance services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Licensee shall pay to Licensor for maintenance and support and upgrade services provided under the applicable Sublicense or other written maintenance and support agreement with or approved by Licensee for each of the Licensed Products a fee equal to 50% of all revenues received (without deduction for value added tax, if any) by Licensee from a Redistributor or Customer relating to maintenance and support services or services for Upgrades or upgrades of systems for such Licensed Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Licensee pays Licensor a percentage of revenue for licensed products to both redistributors and customers. The changed text removes the obligation to pay Licensor a percentage of revenue from *customers*, stating that Licensee retains 100% of customer revenue. However, the contradicted text (b) still requires Licensee to pay Licensor a percentage for maintenance and support/upgrade services provided to *customers*. Since the Licensee retains all revenue from customers under the modified text but still owes the Licensor a percentage of maintenance revenue from customers according to the contradicted text, a direct contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The relationship existing between Licensee and Licensor is one of an independent contractor, and this Agreement shall not be construed as creating a partnership, joint venture, agency relationship or as granting a franchise under federal or any state law.
perturbation.changed_text: The relationship existing between Licensee and Licensor is one of a joint venture, and this Agreement shall not be construed as creating a partnership, independent contractor, agency relationship or as granting a franchise under federal or any state law.
perturbation.explanation: By defining the relationship as a 'joint venture', it introduces conflict because it contradicts the explicit statement that the Agreement should not be construed as creating such a relationship, and conflicts with the parties acting as independent contractors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The relationship existing between Licensee and Licensor is one of a joint venture, and this Agreement shall not be construed as creating a partnership, independent contractor, agency relationship or as granting a franchise under federal or any state law. Each of Licensee and its officers, employees or other representatives shall not enter into or attempt to enter into any obligation on behalf of Licensor. Licensee shall not make any representations to any Redistributors or Customers with respect to the Licensed Products and Documentation, including without limitation representations as to any warranty, covenant or other terms or conditions relating to licensing of the Licensed Products, unless such representations are made (a) in strict accordance with this Agreement or (b) with the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text defines the relationship as a 'joint venture', directly contradicting the rest of the clause stating the Agreement should *not* be construed as creating such a relationship, which is repeated verbatim in the contradicted text. This creates a fundamental conflict regarding the legal nature of the relationship established by the contract, and directly contradicts the 'not be construed as creating a joint venture' part of both texts. It qualifies as a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.
